desires: Stop the shame spiral of my paralysis; I need to force a single action on the scholarship before it becomes permanent failure.

goals.description: Stop overthinking the 'perfect' opening sentence and type exactly one paragraph of the scholarship essay to break the paralysis loop.
goals.rationale: My achievement value (0.5) demands immediate output validation; sitting here feeling like a fraud is useless energy. With a short planning horizon, I don't care about perfection, only securing tangible progress before Thursday so I can stop spiraling.

goals.description: Send the boundary text to Cohort_3 ('Deep work until Thu PM; do not ping') to reclaim control and silence the anxiety of looking unreliable.
goals.rationale: My assertiveness (8) requires me to set these boundaries directly now rather than hoping for silence. Protecting my focus capacity is essential if I want to actually finish this application without looking like a liability to my peers.

goals.description: Eat a quick meal or drink water immediately if my typing stops working from fatigue, otherwise ignore it and push through to secure the 'in progress' status.
goals.rationale: Sleep discipline is low (3) and health priority is secondary (0.13) right now. The fear of missing the scholarship window outweighs the physical cost of pushing through exhaustion. I will address food only if the typing literally stops working.
